name: Xp22_3_distal_NLGN4_KAL1
description: Xp22.3 distal/contiguous gene-deletion region — encompasses STS, KAL1 (ANOS1; Kallmann syndrome), NLGN4 (autism), VCX cluster paralogs. Recurrent deletions are mediated by VCX-A/-B/-C SD blocks at >99% identity. Continuous-gene-deletion phenotypes vary by extent: STS only (X-linked ichthyosis), STS+KAL1 (with Kallmann), STS+NLGN4 (with autism/ID), large deletions also remove TBL1X, GPR143 (ocular albinism). Complements existing Xp22_3_STS.json with the broader contiguous-gene context.

architecture.internal_structure: Four VCX paralogs (VCX-A, VCX-B, VCX-C, VCX2/VCY-A) at >99% identity, organized in tandem at Xp22.3; pseudoautosomal-1 boundary nearby. Critical contiguous genes: STS (steroid sulfatase), KAL1 (ANOS1), NLGN4X (autism), GPR143 (ocular albinism), HCCS, MID1.

sources: ClinGen Dosage Sensitivity NLGN4X, ANOS1, STS
